_CONFIGURE_node_type_id.description: Choose a memory-optimized instance for your cloud provider

_IMPORTANT_autoscaling: MUST be disabled for RTM - do NOT add min_workers/max_workers or autoscale block

_CONFIGURE_cloud_attributes.description: Add ONE of these blocks based on your cloud provider (remove the others)

_IMPORTANT_photon: Photon is NOT supported with RTM - do NOT set runtime_engine to PHOTON

_comment_spark_conf.spark.sql.shuffle.partitions: Keep low (8-16) for sub-second latency. Increase for higher throughput.
REQUIRED: Enables Real-Time Mode for sub-second latency

_comment_requirements.runtime: DBR 16.4 LTS or later required for Real-Time Mode
_comment_requirements.compute: DEDICATED (single-user) compute ONLY - serverless and shared NOT supported
_comment_requirements.access_mode: Use SINGLE_USER so the notebook can checkpoint to Unity Catalog Volumes under /Volumes/...
_comment_requirements.autoscaling: MUST be DISABLED - RTM requires fixed cluster size
_comment_requirements.photon: NOT SUPPORTED with RTM - do not enable Photon
_comment_requirements.instance_type: Memory-optimized instances recommended for streaming workloads
_comment_requirements.workers: Fixed worker count - calculate: source_partitions + shuffle_partitions per stage
_comment_requirements.slot_sizing: Example: 8 Kafka partitions + 20 shuffle = 28 task slots needed

_setup_instructions: 1. Copy this file and remove all keys starting with '_' (comments)
2. Replace 'REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_INSTANCE_TYPE' with an instance from your cloud
3. Replace 'REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_USER_EMAIL' with the user who will own the single-user cluster
4. Add the appropriate cloud_attributes block for your provider (aws_attributes, azure_attributes, or gcp_attributes)
5. Import via Databricks UI: Compute > Create Cluster > JSON Configuration
6. Or use CLI: databricks clusters create --json @cluster_config.template.json
